message:
fix(tests): add missing cancel_runs >= timed_runs assertion in workload simulation conformance test

The workload_simulation_produces_metrics test asserted that cancel_runs >= ready_runs
but was missing the analogous cancel_runs >= timed_runs check, leaving a gap in
priority lane ordering validation. The cancel tier has highest priority and
should always receive at least as many scheduling slots as any lower-priority
lane including the timed tier.

Also updates beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1z5a.15","title":"bd-1z5a support: backfill real rch build IDs into operator E2E artifacts","description":"Why this exists:
The bd-1z5a operator E2E bundle currently records stage commands, exit codes, and event-family evidence, but the machine-readable build provenance is still weak: operator_e2e_bundle.json and operator_e2e_log.jsonl carry null build_id values, and the operator checker only requires summary.build_ids to be a list instead of proving real offloaded build ids were captured. That leaves a truthful functional bundle but an avoidable provenance gap for acceptance criteria 5, 6, and 8.

What this task must do:
- recover the exact rch build ids for the existing successful operator E2E stage commands where possible,
- update the operator harness/checker/tests/artifacts so the bundle fails closed if build provenance silently drops back to null on stages that came from rch lanes,
- keep scope on the operator harness/checker/artifact files and stay off the core verifier Rust files plus NavyRiver's replacement-gap evidence-pack lane.

Acceptance criteria:
1. operator_e2e_bundle.json and operator_e2e_log.jsonl carry concrete build ids for the rch-backed stage entries instead of all-null provenance.
2. operator_e2e_summary.json / .md surface those recovered build ids consistently.
3. scripts/check_verifier_replay_operator_e2e.py and its unit tests fail closed if the required stage build provenance disappears again.
4. Scope stays on tests/e2e, scripts/tests, and operator E2E artifacts only.","notes":"Prepared support lane: telemetry provenance rows recovered and posted in bd-1z5a thread; no edits landed because NavyRiver holds wildcard reservation on operator_e2e artifact files. Keep scope to the narrow compile unblock so bd-1z5a.14 tractability benchmark collection can proceed via rch on the current tree.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"NavyRiver","created_at":"2026-03-11T10:28:09.747340179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T10:45:50.219970977Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T10:45:50.219948415Z","close_reason":"No-op: compile blocker was already fixed in the shared franken_engine worktree before patching","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["replacement-critical","section-10-17"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T10:28:09.747340179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z5a.17","title":"[bd-1z5a support] Reject zero-length replay capsule freshness windows","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs::replay_capsule_freshness_window_valid` currently accepts `issued_at == expires_at` because it allows a freshness window of `0..=3600` seconds and only checks `now < expires_at`. That means a replay capsule with no positive validity interval is treated as fresh if the shared timestamp is still in the future.

What this bug must do:
- make replay capsule freshness fail closed for zero-length windows
- keep the check deterministic and aligned with existing expiry behavior
- add focused inline regressions for zero-length and positive-length cases

Acceptance criteria:
1. `replay_capsule_freshness_window_valid` rejects `expires_at <= issued_at`.
2. `VerifierEconomyRegistry::verify_capsule_integrity` rejects zero-length freshness windows with `FreshnessWindowInvalid`.
3. Inline tests cover the zero-length window rejection and preserve a positive future window success case.
4. Cargo-heavy validation uses `rch` only.","notes":"Robot triage update:
- `bv --recipe actionable --robot-plan` still ranks `bd-1z5a` as highest-impact track
- actionable tracks shown: `bd-1z5a`, `bd-1z5a.17`, `bd-24h7`, `bd-24h7.21`
- non-overlapping actionable lane already assigned to me remains `bd-1z5a.17`, so continuing this shard to closeout instead of claiming a new overlapping bead

Validation status remains feature-aware because `verifier_economy` is gated behind `extended-surfaces`. Latest follow-up fix requires `issued_at <= now < expires_at`.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CrimsonOtter","created_at":"2026-03-12T20:30:11.652018788Z","created_by":"CrimsonOtter","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:06:37.764507456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T20:30:11.652018788Z","created_by":"CrimsonOtter","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1z5a.18","title":"[bd-1z5a support] Reject malformed expected_output_hash in universal verifier capsules","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/connector/universal_verifier_sdk.rs::validate_manifest` only checks `expected_output_hash` for non-emptiness. A capsule signed by a trusted key but carrying a nonsense expected hash like `wrong_hash` is therefore treated as an ordinary replay divergence instead of an invalid capsule. That blurs malformed-capsule rejection with genuine replay mismatch and weakens the expected-result commitment surface in the highest-impact verifier lane.

What this task must do:
- require `expected_output_hash` to be a real sha256-shaped digest during manifest validation
- preserve the ability for valid-but-wrong digests to yield replay `Fail` verdicts
- add focused tests for malformed-hash rejection and valid-shaped divergence

Acceptance criteria:
1. `validate_manifest` rejects malformed `expected_output_hash` values instead of accepting any non-empty string.
2. `replay_capsule` rejects malformed expected hashes before replay/signature-verdict evaluation.
3. A valid-shaped but wrong expected hash still produces a deterministic `Fail` verdict, not a manifest error.
4. Cargo-heavy validation uses `rch` only and stays scoped to the universal verifier SDK target.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BronzeCliff","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:45.180895461Z","created_by":"BronzeCliff","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:45.180895461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.18","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:45.180895461Z","created_by":"BronzeCliff","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z5a.2","title":"bd-1z5a support: BrownCove replacement-gap evidence pack and replay fixture index","description":"Create the missing replacement-gap artifact pack for bd-1z5a without touching RoseMountain's reserved verifier Rust files. Scope: build artifacts/replacement_gap/bd-1z5a/verification_evidence.json, verification_summary.md, and replay_fixture_index.json from current checker coverage, conformance fixtures, and rch evidence; document the current external replay/trust-score verification state and build IDs; coordinate in thread bd-1z5a.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCove","created_at":"2026-03-09T18:10:36.880900642Z","created_by":"BrownCove","updated_at":"2026-03-09T18:18:15.482724260Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T18:18:15.482700787Z","close_reason":"Created replacement-gap evidence pack and replay fixture index","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T18:10:36.880900642Z","created_by":"BrownCove","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z5a.3","title":"bd-1z5a support: operator E2E replay/quarantine/scoreboard bundle","description":"Create a deterministic operator-facing E2E support bundle for bd-1z5a without touching RoseMountain's reserved verifier Rust files. Scope: add an rch-backed shell harness that exercises existing targeted tests for successful replay verification, forged capsule rejection, counterexample witness extraction, quarantine replay, and scoreboard event emission; emit structured CAPSULE_VERIFY_* / VERIFIER_SCORE_* logs with required fields into artifacts/replacement_gap/bd-1z5a/; add a Python checker and unit tests that validate the bundle.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCove","created_at":"2026-03-09T18:23:25.549060283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:35:49.245333312Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:35:49.227011794Z","close_reason":"Completed operator E2E bundle normalization; final artifacts pass checker with single trace and five passing stages","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T18:23:25.549060283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z5a.4","title":"bd-1z5a support: clear SSRF unit-test compile blocker for operator E2E","description":"Unblock bd-1z5a.3 operator E2E remote harness by clearing the current frankenengine-node lib-test compile blocker in crates/franken-node/src/security/ssrf_policy.rs and re-running focused rch-backed validation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCove","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:02:17.796791113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:09:52.939276057Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:09:52.939245250Z","close_reason":"Current tree compiles under fresh rch syncs; prior ssrf_policy failure was stale session state","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:02:17.796791113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -279,7 +279,13 @@ fn workload_simulation_produces_metrics() {
     assert!(timed_runs > 0, "Timed tier must receive scheduling slots");
     assert!(ready_runs > 0, "Ready tier must receive scheduling slots");
 
-    // Cancel tier should have the most or equal runs (highest priority).
+    // Cancel tier should have the most or equal runs versus lower-priority lanes.
+    assert!(
+        cancel_runs >= timed_runs,
+        "Cancel runs ({}) should be >= Timed runs ({})",
+        cancel_runs,
+        timed_runs
+    );
     assert!(
         cancel_runs >= ready_runs,
         "Cancel runs ({}) should be >= Ready runs ({})",
